reason: The statement applies because in this text fragment, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who are Jews, are protected by God while facing punishment for refusing to worship the golden image set up by King Nebuchadnezzar. They are thrown into a burning fiery furnace but are not harmed, and Nebuchadnezzar acknowledges the intervention of a divine being, referring to the fourth person in the fire as 'the Son of God.' This demonstrates that even in the face of punishment, God protects his faithful servants.
